name: Peter's Cream of Pumpkin Soup
recipeIngredient: farmer's cheese, cream cheese or ricotta
½ cup Parsley, chervil and chives
recipeInstructions:
This Thanksgiving soup has only 65 calories per serving.
In a large saucepan, heat the oil over low heat. Add the vegetables (including the fresh pumpkin but not the canned), thyme and bay leaf. Reduce the heat, cover, and let cook gently for 8 minutes. Stir occasionally to keep the vegetables from browning. Add the broth and the pumpkin if using canned; bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer uncovered for either 20 minutes (fresh pumpkin) or 10 minutes (canned pumpkin). Puree the mixture in batches in a blender. Pour the purée back into the saucepan and return to a boil. If it seems too thick, thin with a little hot broth or water. (This dish can be made up to this point and refrigerated, loosely covered, for up to 2 days.) Before serving, reheat the soup to boiling. Return to the blender and add the cottage cheese and powdered milk. Blend for 20 seconds. Taste for seasoning. Ladle into 4 heated bowls and sprinkle with 2 tablespoons of the minced fine herbs before serving.
